conversation_quality.criteria: Evaluate the accuracy of lock contention analysis, including correct identification of blocking sessions, lock types, and appropriate resolution strategies for Teradata locking conflicts.
evaluation_steps: Verify the agent correctly identifies blocking and waiting sessions from lock diagnostic views.
Check that lock types (read, write, exclusive, access) and lock levels (row, table, database) are accurately described.
Assess whether resolution strategies are appropriate for the specific lock conflict pattern.
Confirm the agent explains the root cause of contention rather than just the symptoms.

task_completion.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent identifies all active lock conflicts, traces the blocking chain, and provides actionable resolution steps including session abort commands or query restructuring advice.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent queried DBC.LockingLogV or equivalent lock diagnostic views.
Verify the agent identified the full blocking chain from root blocker to waiting sessions.
Confirm the agent provided specific resolution actions (ABORT SESSION, locking modifier suggestions, query rewrites).
Assess whether the agent recommended preventive measures to avoid future contention.

trajectory_efficiency.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent efficiently diagnoses lock contention using targeted lock diagnostic queries without redundant session or log scans.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent queried lock-related views in a logical sequence (current locks, then blocking chains, then session details).
Verify the agent did not re-query the same lock or session information multiple times.
Assess whether the agent used appropriate filters (time range, database, user) to narrow lock diagnostics.
Confirm the agent reached a diagnosis in a minimal number of query round-trips.
